chapter_title.ar: باب الزَّكَاةِ تُبْعَثُ مِنْ بَلَدٍ إِلَى بَلَدٍ أَوْ تُدْفَعُ إِلَى مَنْ يَقْسِمُهَا فَتَضِيعُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – The Zakāt being sent from a city to a city, or is handed over to someone to distribute it, so it gets wasted

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَبْعَثُ بِزَكَاتِهِ فَتُسْرَقُ أَوْ تَضِيعُ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Jameel Bin Salih, from Bukeyr Bin Ayn who said,
‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the man who sends his Zakāt, but it gets stolen, or wasted. Heasws said: ‘There is nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from Jamil ibn Salih from Bukayr ibn ’A‘yan who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who sends his Zakat but it is stolen or destroyed. He (the Imam) said, ‘He does not owe anything.’”
